step_2_persona_qa.method: agent-simulated 4-persona decadal-close Q&A (D15 allocation: IA + Solo Dev + Educator + Enterprise Architect)

q1_mission_complete.question: Did D15 deliver its consolidation goal — one shared primitive for all 5 role pages with no IA/SEO loss?
q1_mission_complete.answer: Yes. All 5 role pages wrap <PersonaPage> over DocumentationLayout; the 5x buildTechArticleJsonLD boilerplate + 5x byte-identical .lede rule are collapsed to 1x each in the component; every route keeps its sections, URL, and byte-for-byte SEO (override matrix 2/3). The visual language is unified (global .prose + centralized .lede) and now documented as a contract on the primitive. 10/10 cycles, build steady 155 pages.

q2_honest_outcome.question: Was the headline 'consolidation' win real, given the pages were already lean post-D12?
q2_honest_outcome.answer: The real win is structural DRY + a reusable primitive, not a big line cut — and the spec said so up front (re-baselined at cycle 131). Pages dropped 538 -> 457 (-15.1%); net code is -10 lines once the +71 component is counted, but the duplication eliminated (5xJSONLD + 5x.lede -> 1x) and the forward-compat win (a 6th role page is consistent by construction) are the substance. Honest accounting beats inflating the percentage.

q3_lightweight_governance.question: Is a lightweight 5-line AAR (no Reviewer Lens Pass) the right governance weight for D15?
q3_lightweight_governance.answer: Yes — Campaign SO #11 reserves the full 5-persona Reviewer Lens Pass for D11/D14/D17/D20. D15 is a non-RLP code/refactor decadal; a 5-line AAR + the close cascade (mission spec + campaign master + STATE Op-3) is proportionate. Over-governing a render-layer refactor would be its own bloat.

q4_next_decadal.question: What should D16 carry forward from D15?
q4_next_decadal.answer: The estimate-drift finding: the theme-set's per-decadal line/visual expectations were set before D12 trimmed the pages, so D16 (Reference & Glossary Streamline) should re-baseline its own targets against current measured state at its cycle-1 audit, not the theme-set estimate. And the visual contract pattern: where a global stylesheet already unifies the look, codify the contract rather than add component CSS.

step_3_decide.change_selected: CLOSE the mission — append Mission Close Notes + Lightweight 5-line AAR to the spec; flip status to completed; update campaign master M5.6 row + amendments; run STATE Op-3 archive-on-close + repoint Next Session Prompt to M5.7/D16. Non-RLP: no full decadal AAR file, no Reviewer Lens Pass.
